notes: v1.14.32 — photoreal Earth: the landing globe is now a real NASA Blue Marble / Black Marble / BMNG-cloud textured WebGL sphere (2048x1024 day+night, 1024x512 greyscale clouds, WebP-compressed and inlined ~402KB to stay fully offline). A new UV-sphere mesh + day/night/cloud/atmosphere shader (gprog) draws UNDER the arcs/hubs, reusing the same uRotY/tilt/uScale/uCenter projection so the network lines up pixel-for-pixel. Retired the 2D ocean-ball gradient (drawUnderlay keeps only the outer halo + atmosphere rim) and gated off the SPECTRUM land/border point-cloud (SHOW_SPECTRUM_LAND=false) — the texture supplies land + city lights. Hold-to-enter, planets, stars, nebula, and stats unchanged. Verified offline: continents/terminator/clouds/rim render across rotation, no GL errors, node --check 3/3 + sw.js PASS, stamps lockstep v1.14.32. Next (Ship 2): rebalance arc/hub brightness to read over the bright day side + add the sun-horizon glow.
